title: trifolium involucratum var. heterodon
intro: Trifolium involucratum var. heterodon, also known as the heterodon clover, is a type of plant that belongs to the legume family and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Trifolium involucratum var. heterodon include its ability to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as diarrhea and nausea, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile remedy for various health conditions.
